entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 31: Cloud Identity Services - Identity Directory Service
entry.content:
In this video, we explain how cloud identity services handle authorizations using the identity directory service. The process involves choosing between using a user store or not, with the latter relying on corporate identity providers for group mapping. By enabling the user store, the system uses Cloud Identity's authentication service and enriches claims with additional properties such as user groups. In the video we discuss two ways to manage authorizations: through AD/LDAP/EntraID/... groups or Cloud Identity service user groups, offering flexibility for organizations in handling user access.

Takeaways

  • Cloud Identity Service can act as a proxy or use a user store for authorizations.
  • Without a user store, authorization relies on AD/LDAP/EntraID/... groups from a corporate identity provider.
  • Enabling the user store allows for the use of Cloud Identity Service for both authentication and authorization.
  • When the user store is enabled, user groups from Cloud Identity Service are used to map BTP Role Collections.
  • Corporate identity provider is used purely for authentication when the user store is enabled.
  • Using the identity directory service with a user store enriches claims from the corporate identity provider.
  • User store enables more direct control over user authorizations without relying on external groups.
  • Two options for managing authorizations in BTP: AD/LDAP/EntraID/... groups or Cloud Identity Service user groups.
  • Enabling the user store ensures more granular and integrated control over user identity and roles.

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: Keeping up to date with tariff changes
entry.content:
In our new global trade reality, keeping track of tariff changes can seem an insurmountable task. Trade policies are changing more rapidly than ever before and being enforced at a moment’s notice; requiring flexibility and adaptability from companies to implement new tariffs to ensure that they are not penalised for underpaying duties.

If continuously monitoring customs authorities’ official communication channels is the most straight forward option, it comes with great resource constraint. And a certain dose of expertise to determine which change applies to the organization, in what context, etc.

Another option is engaging with a licensed customs broker. These experts would be able to provide the level of monitoring and guidance mentioned above, on behalf of the organization.

But there is a third option for companies wishing to do it themselves and leverage these uncertain times to gain a competitive advantage by reacting faster and more precisely: aligning best-in-class content with best-in-class technology from SAP Global Trade Services to get information in real time and directly connected to the core processes and supply chain.

 

How can companies get the latest duty rates?

 

As per leading analyst firm Gartner, there are two types of global trade management software providers: those that do and those that do not provide content with their tools.

On this matter, SAP has strategically decided to work with its ecosystem of professional global trade data partners offering subscription-based access to a wide selection of trade content. Content from dedicated teams that specialize in identifying, interpreting, and collecting changes and updates to trade regulations that can be imported into SAP through standardized interfaces, but, more importantly, content that can be tailored to each customer’s specific needs in terms of geographic coverage (regions and countries to monitor), content types, and process areas.

This provides organizations with the freedom to select a global trade data partner covering their specific requirements or even combine content from various providers.

As a result, the global trade data partner acts as a central source for up-to-date information helping companies navigate evolving trade regulations. Information provided includes, but not limited to, customs tariff measures and duty rates, export control lists, rules for preferential origin determination for free trade agreements, and more.

Companies such as Mendel Verlag started decades ago with production and publication of customs tariffs and other publications and have expanded to electronic formats that can be directly consumed by global trade management platforms such as SAP Global Trade Services.

To produce this foreign trade content, Mendel Verlag follows a 5-step approach – all done internally by a team of specialists:

  1. Research for the identification and review of relevant sources
  2. Compile information from step 1
  3. Evaluate for relevance and validity
  4. Consolidate different information and sources for integrated data
  5. Quality control including classic editing but also manual checks of electronically processed data

This last step is crucial, as only with expert review can the content provider ensure credibility and reliability of the trade compliance master data information. As highlighted by Mendel Verlag: “Quality is a key requirement of our work. Only valid data can be used effectively in foreign trade and customs processes.”

Once subscribed to a data content service, customers will receive continuous updates.

To ensure that the trade compliance information in SAP Global Trade Services is in sync and therefore also continuously up to date, the procedure is quite simple. The updated file from the content provider – usually in an XML format, is placed on a secure file transfer protocol (SFTP) folder where it is picked up by SAP Global Trade Services and values are uploaded into the platform.  This process includes control parameter checks, and errors can be manually reviewed.

If a company decides to change data providers, and therefore need to replace the previous data provider's content with the new data provider's content, SAP Global Trade Services can of course enable the configuration of time-dependency of this content. This flexibility enables customers to enter two competing sets of content in the system, and when they activate the new data provider's content, the old data provider's content – and all dependent items, is deactivated automatically.

 

Typical misconceptions

 

We, at SAP, strongly believe that working hand-in-hand with a global trade data partner provider is in the best interest of our customers to be able to freely choose, change or combine content from various content providers, addressing specific countries or regions, content types and process areas.

This can raise a few questions and misconceptions:

 

Misconception

Reality

It is more expensive to have the content and the software from different providers

The cost of the content itself is much less than the cost of running a global trade management system, and you pay for it, even when bundled in “one software package”

It is more hassle to manage

Once the one-time setup is completed, updates will flow via certified and tested interfaces with little to no manual intervention (only in case of errors or exceptions)

This approach constraints customers

Quite the opposite, it offers the freedom to pick and choose the best provider for the requirement (compliance area, countries where the company has trade operations, industry, etc.)

SAP recommends this because it is not a content provider

This is absolutely true! In the area of trade compliance, SAP is not a content provider. SAP strategically chose to work with external, specialized, third-party vendors. SAP focuses on delivering the necessary system logic, the workflow, and the integration with core systems and business processes

As eloquently stated by Christian Stadler – Chief Product Owner for SAP International Trade Management portfolio: “working with content partners such as Mendel Verlag makes our SAP solutions even more relevant, and constantly adapted to rapidly changing situations”

If you are interested in discovering more about SAP Global Trade Services, see our dedicated website and associated resources: videos, customer stories, and more.

I would like to thank Mendel Verlag for their collaboration on this article.

Originally published on June 30, 2025 & reposted on this platform

  2. Assign this newly created ACR to Valuation Class in OMSK
  3. Also, assign this ACR to material type in OMSK

By doing so, users can only use the available Valuation Class

entry.title: Re: Sap Analytics Cloud - Security for an end user to be...
entry.content:
@BeatrizDuenas If the rejection is due to DAC. I think you need to examine the DAC. Couple of things you could do to better isolate the issue.

  • Try importing the data using a normal import job. Examine the rejected records.
  • Try importing the data without DAC on model
  • Try importing by by adjusting the DAC. Also as DAC is based on dimension member. Try loading data for only the member you have write access given.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Creating a Fiori tile for ABAP reports
entry.content:
TL;DR – How to create a tile on the SAP Fiori launchpad to run an ABAP report – based on customer experiences. This is a gap-filling technique that is useful for moving existing custom ABAP reports in system conversion projects, or where an SAP predefined report is missing from the SAP Fiori apps reference library.   Alternatives are given at the end of the blog post..

You have an existing ABAP report that you want to run from the SAP Fiori launchpad.  How do you create a tile to run an ABAP report? In the example in this blog post you will see how you can create a tile using the predefined transaction SA38. This is a simple 3-step approach for a sandbox environment. For a development environment, you should consider creating a custom transaction. You will find a brief discussion at the end of this blog post in section Alternative approaches to consider.

 

Creating a tile from scratch is a 3-step process:

  1. Define the tile to launch the ABAP report.
  2. Reference the tile definition into your custom business role for the user.
  3. Adjust the role’s menu and authorizations for any authorizations needed to run the ABAP report.

Once you test your tile you can consider optional Next Steps.

Why create a tile for an ABAP report?

When transitioning from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, you're shifting from using SAP GUI to the SAP Fiori launchpad. This change lets you take advantage of new features offered by RISE with SAP S/4HANA via SAP Fiori apps. You've identified these new functionalities using tools like SAP Signavio. Refer to SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Finding business process improvements with SAP Signavio

Due to time and budget constraints, it's often not feasible to convert all existing user interfaces into SAP Fiori apps. You want to avoid creating a mixed experience for users; instead, you want them to access all applications—both new and old—from a single platform.

The SAP Fiori launchpad enables you to integrate classic UIs, including GUI Transactions, Web Dynpro ABAP applications, WebClient UIs, and ABAP Reports. Many SAP GUI transactions and ABAP Reports are already available in the SAP Fiori apps reference library by filtering on All Apps for SAP S/4HANA.

However, custom ABAP "Z" reports and some SAP predefined ABAP reports, like batch-scheduled ones, may not be listed. For these situations, you might need to manually create a tile in the SAP Fiori launchpad.

Hints:

  • Refer to SAP Help - SAP Fiori launchpad - Administration Guide for how to create a tile for any GUI transaction. 
  • If you are not familiar with the terms technical catalog, business catalog, business role, or Launchpad App Descriptor Item (LADI), you can find these explained in the SAP Help – SAP Fiori launchpad – Administration Guide – Setting up Launchpad Content – About Launchpad Content
  • All screenshots shown on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 system.  This same process can be used on SAP S/4HANA 2020 or higher.

Example Use Case

For customers using Project and Portfolio Management, it can be helpful to extend project roles such as Project Financial Controller (technical id SAP_BR_PROJ_FIN_CONTROLLER) with some additional ABAP reports that are not yet included in the SAP Fiori apps reference library.  

For example, report /RPM/FICO_INT_PLANNING_V2 is an ABAP report that can be scheduled, or optionally run manually. To run the report manually you want a tile on the SAP Fiori launchpad. You use this report to control when and how data from SAP Financials and Controlling (FI-CO) is a applied to projects managed by Portfolio and Project Management. 

Prerequisites

Assumptions:

  • Your SAP Fiori launchpad for SAP S/4HANA is already configured and active. If this has not been done, refer to Learning Video - Activating SAP Fiori Launchpad Using SAP Fiori Foundation Task List

You will need to know:

  • The ABAP report id.  This could be a SAP predefined ABAP report or a custom ABAP report.
  • The technical catalog that will hold the tile definition.
  • The business catalog that will hold the reference to the tile definition.
  • Which business role contains the business catalog.
  • A test user id assigned to the business role that will contain the tile.
  • 1 cross-client workbench transport request
  • 1 client-specific customizing transport request
  • Package Id for your launchpad content.

Pro-tip: It's helpful to keep all SAP Fiori launchpad content in the same package.

For the example use case:

  • Custom technical catalog Z_TC_PROJ_FIN_EXTRA has been created to hold custom apps for Project Financials.
  • Custom business catalog Z_BC_PROJ_FIN_PORTFOLIO has been created to collect custom apps for Project Finance – Portfolio Management
  • The business catalog Z_BC_PROJ_FIN_PORTFOLIO has already been assigned to custom business role Z_BR_PROJ_FIN_CONTROLLER.

You will need access to Fiori launchpad content administration tools including:

  • Launchpad App Manager
  • Launchpad Content Manager Client-Specific
  • Role Maintenance (transaction PFCG)
  • Report execution transaction SA38

Hint: For this example, the Fiori Administration role (id Z_FIORI_FOUNDATION_ADMIN) has been used. This business role is generated by task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4. Refer to Learning Video - Activating SAP Fiori Launchpad Using SAP Fiori Foundation Task List generated by task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4. Authorizations to execute transactions PFCG and PFCGUPDATEROLEMENU were added to the admin role. In practice, the role maintenance tasks would be run by a security administrator user.

Step 1 – Define the tile for the ABAP Report

First you need to define the tile for the SAP Fiori launchpad.

  • You define the tile by creating Launchpad App Descriptor Item (LADI). A LADI contains the tile definition that defines how the tile should appear, and the target mapping that defines which app is launched when the tile is selected in the launchpad.  
  • You define the tile once, and then later you reference the tile to one or more business roles. You typically define the tile in your Sandbox or Development system, then transport the changes to subsequent systems in your landscape as needed.
  • LADIs are created within cross-client technical catalogs. Because technical catalogs are cross-client, you will use a Workbench Transport Request to capture your changes ready for transport. Refer to SAP Help – SAP Fiori launchpad – Administration Guide – Setting up launchpad content - Setting Up Technical Catalogs with the SAP Fiori Launchpad App Manager

Login as your Fiori launchpad content administrator.

Open the Launchpad App Manager.

On the tab Technical Catalogs, find and open the technical catalog that will contain the LADI definition.

Hint: You can optionally create a new standard catalog using the button New Standard Catalog on the same tab.

Select the option Add App > Transaction to add a new LADI for a transaction.

You will be asked to confirm the Package and Workbench Transport Request to capture your changes to the technical catalog.

For the field Transaction Code, enter SAP GUI transaction SA38.

Pro-tip: You should use transaction SA38 for business users executing reports. To avoid unnecessary authorizations being generated, do NOT use transaction SE38 which should only be used by developers who create reports.

Press the <ENTER> key. This will default in some information from SE93 such as:

  • Device Type settings
  • Application Component id
  • Default tile name

Adjust the following settings in tab Target Application Fields:

  • Semantic Object – use the closest SAP Semantic object that fits with the main business object of the report.  For the example, because the report is run for project portfolios, the standard semantic object ProjectPortfolio is selected.
  • Action – The action for the Semantic Object is freely definable. Enter a camel case verb that fits with purpose of the report. Action is set to integrateFICO4Planning
  • Target Application Title – this is the link text that is shown whenever the app is displayed as a link, e.g. in Search Results, Related Apps, and List of Links dialogs. For the example use case, Target Application Title is set to Portfolio - Financial Periodic Planning and Integration.
  • Application Component ID – this is the support component shown in the About and App Support dialogs to indicate where to search on SAP for Me for information. For the example, Application Component ID is set to PPM-PFM.

Pro-tip: There are more than 3K Semantic Objects delivered by SAP, with predefined dynamic navigation links to related launchpad contact, including any additional custom tiles you create. Avoid creating custom Semantic Objects as these are not linked to any existing apps, making it harder for users to navigate between apps.

Add the following parameters in tab Parameters:

  • Program – set Default Value to your program id, and set Rename To value  RS38M-PROGRAMM
  • DynproNoFirstScreen  - set Default Value = 1
  • DYNP-OKCODE  - set Default Value = STRT to start the report

To complete the definition, you adjust the tile appearance in the tab Tiles.

Adjust the following settings in tab Tiles:

  • Tile texts - Title, Subtitle, and Information
  • Tile Keywords – these are used to help find the tile when using Search on Apps
  • Tile icon – you can use the value help to search for suitable icons

Select Save to save your LADI.

Step 2 – Assign the tile definition to the business role

Launchpad content items – such as apps - are directly assigned to business catalogs, and indirectly assigned to business roles. Business catalogs typically contain a small subset of closely related apps that are assigned together to one (or more) business roles.  Business catalogs and business roles are client-specific.  

Open the Launchpad Content Manager – Client-Specific.

On the tab Catalogs, find and open the business catalog that will reference the LADI.

  • You can use an existing business catalog, or you can select Create to create a new business catalog.
  • If you create a new business catalog you will need to assign the business catalog to the business role. 

Find and add the LADI to the business catalog using the option Add Tiles/Target Mappings > Add Tile/TMs to selected catalog.

Find your LADI add reference it to your business catalog by selecting Add Tile/TM Reference.

Confirm the LADI has been added to your business catalog.

To check which roles use this business catalog, select Show Usage in Roles.

 

Step 3 – Adjust the business role content and authorizations

You now need to adjust the related business role's content and authorizations.  The easiest way to do this is to use transaction PFCGUPDATEROLEMENU. This transaction runs program PRGN_COMPARE_ROLE_MENU.  You can also adjust the role manually in Role Maintenance transaction PFCG.

Run transaction PFCGUPDATEROLEMENU for the related business role(s).

Any mismatches with the current role content are highlighted by the traffic light in the first column.

Select the mismatched row(s). Adjust the role menu by selecting Adapt Menu.

Press Refresh to review the corrected role content.  Correcting the role content impacts the role's authorization status, which needs to be adjusted for the change in content.

Select Change Role to review and adjust the changes in the role's authorizations. 

Find the new entries for Authorization Object S_PROGRAM in class BC_C. These grant authorization to run the program. 

Adjust the authorization program setting the program id. Set authorization field P_PROGRAM to the program you want the user to run.

Adjust the activities so that the user can run the report. Set authorization field P_ACTION to SUBMIT.  Pro-tip: You can also set VARIANT if you want the user to save variants to minimize data entry. 

Generate the role to update the profile.

Confirm the authorizations profile status is now generated.

Pro-tip: If you want to use the changes immediately you may need to run a User Comparison in the Role Maintenance transaction PFCG. This updates the cache that holds the user's current authorizations. 

Review the User tab.  Make sure your test user is assigned to the role. Run the User Comparison for your test user.

Test your tile

You can now use your test user to test your tile from SAP Fiori launchpad using the App Finder.

Select the tile using your business catalog title or any part of the keywords or tile texts.

Select the tile to launch the report.

Next steps – consider where to place your tile

Once you are satisfied the tile is working, you can decide where to place it to help the user find it during their day-to-day work.  For example, you can add the tile to any Page assigned to any of the role’s spaces.

You can find which space is assigned to your business role using either:

  • Launchpad Content Manager – Client Specific - tab Roles - option Show Spaces
  • Overview of Role, Spaces and Pages (transaction /UI2/RSP_LIST)

Refer to SAP Help – SAP Fiori launchpad – Administration Guide – Analyzing Spaces and Pages

Worth knowing: You maintain spaces in app F4834 Manage Launchpad Spaces. You maintain the related pages in app F4512 Manage Launchpad Pages.  Refer to Learning Video - Creating SAP Fiori Launchpad Layouts for Custom Business Roles.

 

Alternative approaches to consider

For improved usage tracking and role maintenance

Instead of using transaction SA38, you can define a custom transaction for your ABAP report id.  Using a custom transaction enables you to track the usage of this app by transaction code.  It can also be useful for the security administrator.  For example, from Role Maintenance (transaction PFCG) they can search for the custom transaction across all security roles.

You can define custom transactions in transaction SE93.  For example, you could define a report transaction or a parameter transaction.

  • Instead of entering the parameters in the LADI, you enter the parameters in the custom transaction.
  • Mark your transaction as suitable for SAP GUI for HTML so that others know you have already checked all its features work from the SAP Fiori launchpad. 
  • You then use your custom transaction to create your LADI and complete the changes to the business catalog and business role as explained above.

Refer to: SAP Help - ABAP Platform - ABAP Workbench Tools - Maintaining Transactions

For improved report design and behaviour

Consider updating your ABAP reports to newer versions that are optimized for cloud use and mobile devices. If a report is hard to use, lacks information, or could be improved with visuals, consider redesigning it for a better experience.

For example:

You might consider replacing it with a SAP Fiori elements app,  for example, using the floorplans List Report or Analytical List Page. Refer to SAP Design Guidelines.

  • Pro-tip: Make sure your developers are up-to-date with the latest recommended programming models such as Fiori Elements Flexible Programming Model Explorer and ABAP RESTful Programming Model.

Or you might consider replacing it with a SAP Analytics Cloud story, if you want to combine information from SAP S/4HANA and another solution. Refer to Learning Journey - Designing Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Re: Missing selection box column on UI Table ABAP RAP
entry.content:
Totally depends on your requirement then. Using RAP if you want to enable selection of line item, there must be some action you want to perform after selecting the row. If you just want to navigate to object page(detail), clicking on row item should be ok which lets you go to object page. Object page can be enabled using association in your CDS.

if you have an action to be performed via row select, multi select, you must enable action using annotation @ui.DataFieldForAction under @ui.LineItem. Adding this annotation will enable row selection box in front of each rows.

-BR

Tejas

entry.title: Re: Auto Import to PRE-PRD - in ChaRM
entry.content:
Hi Satish,

Regarding your concern, for auto-import during status change, you could use urgent change transaction, if you want to use normal change, then you need to use the preliminary import scenario in order to use the auto-import function during status change. See below documents for details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/8b923a2175be4939816f0981b73856c7/d6e6bcc740964311987d440ab83e6684.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/8b923a2175be4939816f0981b73856c7/48db8900a73b04e7e10000000a42189c.html?locale=en-US

Besides these 2 scenarios, there is no standard way to trigger the auto-import during status change(from QA->Pre-PROD). You will then need to involve consulting service to see if customization is feasible or not.

BR

entry.title: A Step-by-Step Guide to Automatic Account Determination in SAP MM-FI Integration
entry.content:
🔗Why is MM-FI Integration Required in SAP?

In SAP, MM (Materials Management) handles procurement and inventory, while FI (Financial Accounting) manages financial transactions and reporting. Integrating these two modules is essential for real-time, accurate financial accounting of logistics operations.

1. Real-Time Financial Postings

 At the time of Goods Receipt, MM handles the stock update, while FI manages the financial impact. To avoid manual G/L postings during GR, SAP uses automatic account determination to seamlessly integrate MM and FI, ensuring accurate financial entries with minimal effort.

2. Reduced Manual Efforts

Without integration, users would have to manually assign G/L accounts during every transaction, increasing the risk of errors. Integration with Automatic Account Determination (OBYC) eliminates this dependency.

3. Reduced Manual Efforts

Inventory changes in MM (e.g., stock movement, consumption) affect company valuation. Integration ensures the FI module reflects these changes instantly, keeping the balance sheet accurate.

Finally, MM-FI integration is the backbone of seamless financial operations in SAP. It ensures logistics processes are accurately represented in financial books, with minimal manual effort and maximum reliability.

Let's walk through the step-by-step configuration and use-case setup for both material and non-material-based procurement scenarios.

Step 1: Activate Valuation Grouping Code (Transaction: OMWM)

Path: IMG > MM > Valuation and Account Assignment > Account Determination without Wizard > Define Valuation Control

Valuation grouping allows you to group valuation areas (like plants) under a common valuation grouping code, simplifying account determination across plants.

Step 2: Group Valuation Areas (Transaction: OMWM)

Path: IMG > MM > Valuation and Account Assignment > Account Determination without Wizard > Group Together Valuation Areas

Assign multiple plants or valuation areas to the same Valuation Grouping Code. This eliminates the need to maintain separate general ledger (G/L) account configurations for each plant.

💡Example: VG Code 0001 groups plants 1000, 1001, and 1234, all under company code 1000.

Step 3: Define Account Category Reference (Transaction: OMWM)

Path: IMG > MM > Valuation and Account Assignment > Account Determination without Wizard > Define Valuation Classes

Set up Account Category Reference (ACR) - a 4-digit code linking material types with valuation classes. This controls which valuation class can be assigned to which material type.

We have standard valuation classes based on material type. I have copied them and created valuation classes for each material type, assigning them to the account category reference I had previously created.

There is no direct configuration between material type and valuation class account assignment, elaborating which material types related materials can be chosen for which valuation class. In this step, I have assigned my material type with the account reference category, so that the valuation class will get automatically assigned to the valuation class.

Step 4: Assign Account Category Reference to Material Type

Map the Account Category Reference to the Material Type to ensure automatic derivation of the valuation class during material master creation.

✅This reduces manual input and aligns material types with relevant financial postings.

In the Material Master View

Step 5: Define Account grouping for Movement Types (Transaction: OMWM)

Path: IMG > Material Management > Valuation and account assignment > account determination > account determination without wizard > Define Account Groupings for movement types.

Movement types trigger valuation strings, which link to transaction keys and account modifiers. While SAP provides standard movement types, understanding their link to account determination is essential for troubleshooting and custom processes.

NOTE: This is a step to create a movement type, but we do not make any changes or create a movement type, as SAP has provided all the necessary movement types for the business according to the requirements.

Step 6: Try creating a PO and post a GR against the PO

Create a PO and perform a GR to simulate a real scenario.

This helps validate whether the automatic account determination is working correctly before going live.

From the MM perspective, we know only the process but not the transaction and G/L account in the OBYC settings. To identify this, let’s follow the steps below (Automatic Account Posting) for basic understanding.

Step 7: Automatic Account Posting (Transaction: OBYC)

PATH: IMG > Material Management > Valuation and account assignment > Account determination > Account determination without wizard > Configure automatic postings

Let’s try simulating our scenario for plant 1000, Material HR100000, movement type 101 (GR Goods Receipt)

Use simulation tools to verify how SAP derives the accounts based on:

  • Company Code: 1000
  • Valuation Group Code: 0001
  • Material type: ZROH
  • Valuation class: 1100
  • Movement Type: 101
  • Plant: 1000
  • Chart of Accounts: INT

These posting lines indicate the possible scenarios from the FI perspective for mapping the G/Ls concerning the MM process.

For Ex, Here in our scenario, GR Goods receipt will hit transaction BSX – Inventory Posting and WRX -GR/IR Clearing Account.

Go back and navigate to Account Assignment

Here, assign G/L accounts to relevant transaction keys like:

  • BSX – Inventory Posting

  • WRX – GR/IR Clearing Account

Now, try posting GR for PO with movement type 101

Document posted, check for FI documents

Handling Scenarios Without a Material Master

There are times when you need to procure services or materials without using a material master. This requires a different approach for automatic account determination. 

Step 1: Define material Group (Transaction: OMSF)

Path: SPRO > Logistics – General > Material Master > Settings for Key Fields > Define Material Groups

Material groups help categorise non-material items and are later used to derive G/L accounts.

Step 2: Assign the valuation Class to the Material Group

Path: SPRO > IMG > Material management > purchasing > Material master > entry aids for item without material master

This mapping ensures the system uses a material group-based valuation class during procurement.

Step 3: Assign/Check General Modifier to Account Assignment

Path: SPRO > IMG > Material management > Purchasing > account assignment –maintain account assignment categories

Maintain G/L accounts in OBYC under GBB-VBR (Offsetting Entry for Inventory Posting - Consumption) for relevant valuation classes or material groups.

Step 4: Create Purchase Order (Transaction: ME21N)

Use Account Assignment Category "K" (Cost Centre) and select the relevant material group. The system will automatically pick the G/L account based on OBYC settings.

✅Transactions triggered:

  • GBB-VBR for inventory offset

  • KBS for cost centre posting

If you found this guide helpful, don’t miss my other SAP MM blogs covering real-world automation, configuration tips, and best practices for procurement and inventory management.

Click to view other SAP MM WM Blogs - SAP Community

 

Regards,

Mannem Leelamohan

entry.title: Subject: Link in Outlook Calendar Invite Not Loading Correctly in Microsoft Edge
entry.content:
environment:

-Module: Learning Experience
-Action: Booking a course via the three-dot menu
-Browser: Issue in Microsoft Edge, works fine in Google Chrome
-Impacted feature: Embedded calendar link in Outlook calendar invite

Issue Description: When accessing a course within the new Learning Experience interface, users select “Book” using the three-dot menu. This action downloads an Outlook calendar invite containing a link to the course page.

  • In Google Chrome, copy and paste the link and loads the expected page. 

  • In Microsoft Edge, the link displays the correct destination in the address bar, but the page itself fails to load properly. [Either copy and paste or click on the link]

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Navigate to a course within the Learning Experience

  2. Click the three-dot menu and select “Book”

  3. Open the downloaded Outlook calendar invite

  4. Click the embedded calendar link

  5. Observe behavior in both Chrome and Edge

Expected Result: Link should load the course page successfully across all supported browsers.

Actual Result in Edge: The link shows the correct URL, but the page does not load as expected.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere catalog - Harvesting from SAP BW/4HANA
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere introduced in 2023 advanced catalog functions aimed at making data more accessible and enhancing data-driven insights (initial announcement). This capability enables the management, organization and augmentation of metadata extracted from multiple SAP data sources. It aids in the discovery of curated data assets and lets users and data stewards search for and classify these assets. Additionally, the catalog allows to enhance assets with business metadata, supporting organizations in their data governance initiatives. Initially, it integrates with sources like SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Datasphere BW Bridge (feature overview), pulling metadata from various assets within these systems.

SAP BW/4HANA as a comprehensive data warehouse enriched with massive content of business logic and semantics is a crucial element for our customers to unlock the full potential of advanced analytics, machine learning and informed decision-making at scale.

We are very happy today to announce that we are expanding the coverage of SAP Datasphere catalog metadata harvesting with the support of SAP BW/4HANA allowing our customers to run accurate, contextualized, and trustworthy data analysis.

Configuring Connection to SAP BW/4HANA

​First let’s have a look at how we configure SAP Datasphere to connect and retrieve metadata from a SAP BW/4HANA system. This will require to create a live connection as well as reuse this live connection to create a remote system connection entry point for the catalog. Let's start by creating the live connection.

Click System and then Configuration.

In the Data Integration tab, you need to create a live data connection of type tunnel to the SAP BW/4HANA system to import the assets metadata. This live connection can be further reused either in Space Management to import models in modeller or in the remote systems for the metadata extractions for the catalog.

Click "Manage Live Data Connections".

Click "+" to create a new live connection, this will guide you to create this connection with a wizard-like experience.

This will require to fill in the following mandatory information:

  • Host URL
  • HTTPS Port
  • Client Id
  • User Name and Password

Once this live connection is created, we can now directly use it to create a remote system for the purpose of metadata harvesting.

Let's click on Catalog & Marketplace and then Metadata Extractions.

Click "+" to create a new remote system.

And now select the newly introduced entry "SAP BW/4HANA"

Select the Live Connection you just created before and then create this new remote connection.

Now we can edit the remote connection you just created to specify which Info Areas and sub info areas we want harvest the metadata from.

You can also switch to the Source Systems for information to see which source systems are involved in the retrieved metadata.

From then, we can synchronize the extraction of the metadata or define a scheduling job as usual with any of the remote systems the SAP Datasphere catalog can connect to.

The metadata for all the objects associated to the selected Info Areas will be available in the catalog. This includes the following type of containers or assets:

  • Info Areas
  • Source Systems
  • Advanced Datastore Objects
  • Info Objects
  • Data Source
  • Info Sources
  • Composite Providers
  • Transformations
  • BW Query Elements
  • Open ODS Views
  • Open Hub Destinations
  • Data Transfer Processes
  • Aggregation Levels

Exploring SAP BW/4HANA metadata

We can now explore the metadata available in the catalog from the BW/4HANA system we synced with by clicking "Catalog & Marketplace" and then "Search".

We can select the container that corresponds to our BW/4HANA system we harvested the metadata from.

And then expand and navigate through the different Info Areas or Source Systems as containers.

You can also filter by the different types of assets available

Click "Show More" to see the entire list of supported objects that can help you refine what you are searching for.

Opening any of the asset gives access to the technical metadata as well as the column details. Any of the existing feature to enrich the assets with business metadata such as descriptions, business glossary terms, KPIs, and Classification Tags can be used to further enhance the metadata for the SAP BW/4HANA harvested metadata as described in this blog.

Lineage and Impact Analysis graph is available for any of the assets from BW/4HANA when applicable which allows to understand how the objects are created and how they are used, but also offers easy navigation to other assets in the catalog.

By leveraging SAP Datasphere catalog with metadata harvested from SAP BW/4HANA, organizations can ensure that their data is not only accessible but also meaningful and reliable, driving informed decision-making and powering advanced analytics and AI initiatives.

What’s Next?

We will continue to extend the coverage of the SAP metadata we can harvest to continuously support organizations on their data governance journey.

I also want to share some additional resources for you around SAP Datasphere: 

  • Join SAP Datasphere community to see what’s new, ask questions, share your feedback, and more. 
  • SAP Datasphere product overview  
  • Documentation for SAP Datasphere catalog

entry.title: 🚀 SAP RAP Events: Custom Logic Without BAdIs or User Exits
entry.content:
As SAP S/4HANA continues to evolve with clean core principles, the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) is taking center stage. One of the most underused but powerful features of RAP is its event-based architecture, which allows you to plug in custom logic in a clean and upgrade-safe way.

Today, I want to share how you can listen to standard RAP events and trigger your own custom events — no BAdIs or user exits needed!

🧠 The Concept: RAP Event Listeners

In classic SAP ABAP, you’d typically write enhancements using:

  • 🔧 BAdIs

  • 🔄 User exits

  • 💡 Implicit enhancements

But in RAP, you can react to standard business behavior via events. That means you can write your own custom logic that responds when something happens in a standard RAP BO (Business Object) — like a purchase order being approved — by listening to its published events.

This is cleaner, future-proof, and doesn't modify the standard codebase.

🎯 Real Use Case

Let’s say SAP’s standard BO for PurchaseOrder publishes an event when a PO is approved. You want to send a custom email, update another Z table, or trigger integration — only when that event happens.

✅ In RAP, you can do this like so:

  • Step 1: The standard RAP object publishes the approval event (R_PurchaseOrderTP)

  • Step 2: Your custom RAP behavior definition listens for that event.

  • Step 3: You trigger a custom implementation, e.g., a mail sender class or integration call.

No BAdI. No user exit. No modifications.

🖼️ Illustration: RAP Event Flow

Figure 1: Listening to Standard RAP Events in a Custom BO

🛠️ How to Implement

Here’s a simplified code sketch of how this works:

1. Listen to Standard Event in Custom BO

CLASS zcl_po_ext DEFINITION

PUBLIC ABSTRACT FINAL

FOR EVENTS OF R_PurchaseOrderTP.

PUBLIC SECTION.

PROTECTED SECTION.

PRIVATE SECTION.

ENDCLASS.

2. Handle the Event in Your Class

CLASS lhe_po_ext DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.

PRIVATE SECTION.

METHODS on_approved FOR ENTITY EVENT

approved FOR purchaseorder~approved.

ENDCLASS.

You’re done — and you didn’t need to touch a single line of standard code!

💡 Why This Matters

  • 🔒 Clean Core Compliant – Stay upgrade-safe.

  • ⚡ No Enhancements or BAdIs Needed – Simpler architecture.

  • 🧱 Composable – Build logic modularly using RAP principles.

  • 🔁 Reusability – Listen to multiple events, reuse handlers across objects.

📣 Final Thoughts

The RAP event mechanism offers a paradigm shift in how we build custom logic in SAP. It allows us to be event-driven, clean, and compliant with SAP's future direction.

If you’re developing on S/4HANA or looking to modernize your ABAP stack — RAP events are your secret weapon.

👀 Have you tried this approach in your projects? I’d love to hear your experience. Let’s connect and share ideas!

🔗 #SAPRAP #CleanCore #S4HANA #ABAP #SAPDevelopers #NoMoreBADIs #EventDrivenArchitecture

entry.title: Re: Unable to find deposit payment processing capability...
entry.content:
In SAP there is a standard contract type DO01 and DO02 that is specifically used for a "Security Deposit Contract". The disadvantage of this is that you end of with 2 contracts, one for the security deposit and one for the actual lease-out, so I generally don't like this approach.

The Bond scenario will have to be configured if you want to use it only on the lease-out. The quick way is to copy the rent condition type and re-label this as "Bond" and use the same rent flow type or create new flow types. This would be the approach for a demo only.

For a real implementation you must check with the customer what the expectation is regarding journal entries. Bond is generally not captured as a revenue entry so therefor you'll have to check what account is required and also if this should be posted to the default customer recon account or if this requires a special GL indicator to post it to a separate customer recon account to capture "RE Rent Deposits or Bonds".

The second complexity is at the end of the lease. How should the original bond be treated as there are 3 scenarios:

  1. Full refund
  2. Partial refund
  3. No refund

There are different ways to achieve this, but probably the simplest way would be to set the original bond condition type to zero at the end of the lease which will trigger a reversal of the original entry. If any money is to be retained a one-time rent or separate condition type (e.g. Bond Retained) can be added to the contract.

You'll end up with 2 cashflows, one for the full bond return and one for the amount that is to be retained.

There are variations of this setup so it is important to discuss this with the customer.

entry.title: Re: Credit exposure commitment type 500 is not updated wh...
entry.content:
It could be due to

  • billing document created from FWSD might not be triggering the standard credit exposure update logic due to missing integration points
  • Missing or Inactive BAdIs like BADI_SD_CM or UKM_R3_ACTIVATE since Credit Management relies on these
  • Queue Issues which can be checked in SMQ1 for stuck queues like UKM_DOCUMENT_PROCESS

If required, you can execute transactions like UKM_COMMITMENTS, UKM_TRANSFER_ITEMS, or UKM_RVKRED07 to rebuild the credit exposure manually

entry.title: Want to Process the Sales orders against the particular MRP only
entry.content:
Currently, during Sales Order (SO) creation in SAP, the system automatically selects the first available MRP (based on date/month) and deducts quantity from it. This leads to incorrect stock consumption and affects Raw Material (RM) planning.

We require the ability to manually select the MRP record (product-wise, month-wise, or project-wise) during SO creation. The system should then consume quantity only from the selected MRP and not affect any other MRP record. This is necessary to ensure accurate RM requirement planning and avoid consumption mismatches.

entry.title: Re: SAP Sybase ASE Audit Logs: Handling Multi-Row Single...
entry.content:
To handle split extrainfo in Sybase ASE audit logs:
1. Use auditprocess utility or sp_audit parsing scripts to reconstruct events—they handle multi-row extrainfo properly.
2. There’s no supported way to increase extrainfo column length in sysaudits_xx tables.
3. To combine rows manually, use event, eventtime, and objname as correlation keys and STRING_AGG() (if on ASE 16+) or cursor logic in a stored proc.
4. For robust analysis, export logs and process externally (e.g., Python script or shell pipeline).
5. Consider using audit init with larger audit block sizes to reduce row-splitting frequency.

entry.title: Re: Getting short dump FM BAPI_BUS2002_SET_STATUS (Set/Reset Status for BUS2002 (Network))
entry.content: BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT due to CALL SCREEN in function module CO_KO_ORDER_ENTRY_EXT. So, it's RAP but it's not detected as RAP. If it was detected as RAP, there would be no CALL SCREEN. Maybe you forget to call something like BAPI_PS_INITIALIZATION or call it at the wrong moment.

entry.title: Enhance Central Finance error handling with AIF & ISM integration
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s digital landscape, minimizing downtime and streamlining document posting issues and their resolutions are crucial, especially for financial systems. Central Finance (CFIN) has already revolutionized finance operations, but what happens when document replication fails?

Enter the powerful integration of AIF (Application Interface Framework) and ISM (Incident Solution Matching), designed to tackle error handling with automation and intelligence.

Introducing SAP Note 3531918

SAP Note 3531918 unveils a new technique: integrating AIF with ISM for CFIN error handling. This update automates solution identification, enhancing issue resolution efficiency.

What’s new?

The functionality is accessible via the 'Functions' option within the AIF Monitor transaction that applies to each error message.

The new integration seamlessly connects AIF which manages business interface errors in Central Finance with ISM, an AI-powered service/API that matches incidents with relevant SAP solutions in the form of SAP Note, KBA, FAQ Notes etc.

Note:
This enhancement builds upon the framework introduced in SAP-Note 3389718, “Navigating AIF Errors: Central Finance Error Support and Solutions,” and SAP Note 3271558, “Central Finance: Enable AIF Error Resolution Support Content.” It primarily leverages the customization table V_CFIN_AIFKBAURL.

With this enhancement, the solution is now empowered by the ISM API, which utilizes Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Machine Learning (ML) algorithms for context-based intelligent search, enabling a more dynamic AIF error resolution experience.

Key Benefits:

  • Automated Error Diagnosis - Error details are directly pushed from customer ‘s system to the ISM engine, eliminating guesswork.
  • Instant Solution Suggestions - ISM scans SAP’s knowledge base, providing context-specific resolutions automatically.
  • Reduced Manual Work - Users no longer need to search SAP Notes manually. They receive solutions within the CFIN error framework.

How does it work?

Think of it as having an SAP expert on-call 24/7.

Open the /N/AIF/ERR Transaction and click on the function option next to an error message in AIF and choose Add Incident Solution Matching Results.

The system packages technical metadata (like product component, system type, error description) and routes it to ISM via a secure HTTP connection. ISM then searches SAP’s database using NLP and ML, returning tailored recommendations.

The results are added to the Support Information section.

Note: As it is a context-based solution extraction, some of the recommended SAP Notes or suggestions may not be applicable to every customer scenario. It is at the customer's discretion to review, validate, and apply the appropriate solutions as per their system landscape and business process requirements.

Implementation Guide

The following implementation guide applies for systems running CFIN on S4HANA CORE releases 107 and 108 and above (up to specific support package levels).

Prerequisite: Download the Root CA and CPI certificates from the SAP-Note 3531918 attachments.

1. Upload SSL Certificates (Transaction: STRUST)

a) Go to the transaction STRUST.

b) Upload the certificates into your designated transaction STRUST under new PSE folder (e.g., SSL client ISM_S4HANA_ISMS4_DQ).

c) Save your changes.

2. Configure RFC Connection (Transaction: SM59)

a) Create a new HTTP RFC destination - ISM_API_CONNECT

b) Select connection type G (HTTP to External Server).

c) Under Technical Settings:

  • Enter the target host: sapit-dataandintegration2-prod.it-cpi018-rt.cfapps.eu10-003.hana.ondemand.com.
  • Enter the path prefix: /http/GenericInboundHttpRequest?Pid=S4HANA_ISM_IncidentSol-MatchingService.
  • Enter the port number: 443.

d) Under Logon & Security, set up SSL and authentication using your STRUST setup.

3. Test the Connection

a) To validate connectivity, use the Connection Test button in SM59

4. Activate Required BAdI

a) Go to the CFINIMG node.

Ensure the BAdI is already active from this node, if not active, Activate it

b) Select Central Finance: Target System Settings and BAdIs Central Finance.

c) In the BADI list, select Activate Error Resolution Support Content.

d) Select the implementation class FINS_CFIN_AIF_CUSTOM_FUNCTION and check for activation

 

Why This Matters

This integration is more than a technical enhancement. By automating manual processes, it reduces resolution time and improves user experience. For large enterprises, this is a significant advantage in managing complex landscapes and high-volume transactions.

Incorporating AIF and ISM into your Central Finance system is a step towards a more efficient and user-friendly error resolution process. Embrace this innovation and watch your finance operations thrive.

Keywords:

Central Finance, AIF, ISM, SAP S/4HANA, RFC, Error Handling, Automation, STRUST, SM59, BADI

entry.title: GR 相关配置问题咨询
entry.content:
1、配置ID102660,允许组货币作为主要货币是什么意思，其他系统有“科目集成的正确物料组”


2、配置DIGITS，重分类方法中的触发器要怎么配置


3、如果我需要一个以人民币出具的合并报表是不是需要新增一个组货币为CNY的合并分类账

entry.title: Re: GR 相关配置问题咨询
entry.content:
1、配置ID102660,允许组货币作为主要货币是什么意思，

This indicator specifies if it is possible to choose the group currency as the leading currency for selected consolidation units.

  • If you set this indicator, you can decide if the local or the group currency is used as the leading currency during data collection from accounting.
  • If you don't set this indicator, the local currency is automatically used as the leading currency during data collection.

Help: 

Group Currency is Leading Currency: You can specify if the group currency should be used as the leading currency instead of the local currency in data collection. Selecting this option has the following implications 

2、配置101011，重分类方法中的触发器要怎么配置

触发器就是一个选择集合， selection. 可以在Define Selection 定义修改。

Trigger selection 包含的所有item就会被之后的 重分类程序运行。

最好使用标准的，不要太多自定义。 特别是方法不要自定义。

如果只是想修改触发集合， 你直接换个新的集合就行。新建和修改的app都是Define Selection。

3、如果我需要一个以人民币出具的合并报表是不是需要新增一个组货币为CNY的合并分类账.

可以新建一个extension version, Group currency =CNY

More information can be found in  Multiple Group Currencies

entry.title: Re: GRC QA cannot startup after DB Refresh
entry.content:
Hi, looks like the user and/or password maintained in the ABAP SSFS is not matching with the password saved within the database. Validate if the schema owner is really SAPSR3.

Check if changing the password via BR*Tools helps to eliminate this issue with commands below:

brconnect -u / -c -f chpass -o SAPSR3 -p <password> -s abap

or

brconnect -u system/<sys_pwd> -c -f chpass -o SAPSR3 -p <sap_pwd> -s abap -RDB

In case BRT$ADM user for executing BR*Tools command has been set up, enhance the command with another "/" in the command line, as seen below:

brconnect -u // -c -f chpass -o SAPSR3 -p <password> -s abap

entry.title: Transitioning from SAP Design Studio to Advanced Reporting Solutions
entry.content:
The landscape of business intelligence is transforming, and staying ahead means embracing change. With the deprecation of SAP Design Studio, businesses should transition their custom reports to more advanced technologies to ensure seamless data-driven decision-making and meet emerging business needs.

Understanding the Current Situation

SAP Design Studio applications were officially deprecated in the CE2102 release and are no longer available as of CE2108. As this technology is no longer supported, it's crucial for users to explore alternative solutions to recreate their reports. Continuing with Design Studio may lead to operational risks and hinder future innovation. Despite the ability to use custom Design Studio apps, it's strongly advisable to transition to newer technologies that promise support and advancement.

Customer Options for Rebuilding Reports

To support this transition, there are several effective options for rebuilding reports:

  • Rebuild Reports Using Web Dynpro
  • Rebuild Reports Using MDA
  • Rebuild Reports Using Review Booklet

Understanding the purpose and layout can refine your choice:

Web Dynpro

  • Purpose: Used for detailed, flexible analysis and visualization of large data sets.
  • Features: Allow users to switch between grid and graphical displays, with configurable settings.

 

Multidimensional Reports

  • Purpose: Designed for detailed analysis and visualization
  • Features: Enables analysis across multiple dimensions using pivot tables and charts, with functions for slicing, dicing, and saving personal data views

 

Review Booklet

  • Purpose: Offers an aggregated data overview
  • Features: Predefined business pages that focus on presenting key insights

 

Select the appropriate report type according to your specific business requirements.

Exploring the Rebuild Path

Crafting a successful transition involves mapping the path from Design Studio to your chosen method.

  • Rebuild Reports Using Web Dynpro

Web Dynpro provides a straightforward solution for report rebuilding. Customers can easily create web Dynpro reports by creating application in the APP View Browser. Detailed steps can be checked in the demo below or the blog.

DEMO

It offers simplicity and ease of use, making it ideal for those seeking minimal time investment and consultant involvement. However, it's important to note that Web Dynpro does not feature upcoming enhancements or updates, which may influence long-term capability.

  • Rebuild Reports Using MDA

MDA emerges as a compelling option with moderate complexity, promising regular updates and enhancements.

Before creating an MDA report, queries must be recreated in the APP Custom Analytical Queries and Services.

DEMO

Following this, create the MDA report in the APP Manage KPIs and Reports with a few steps—demonstrated in the demo.

DEMO

In a word, As the successor to Web Dynpro, MDA involves initial query recreation, yet balances time, cost, and the need to remain aligned with current trends and capabilities.

  • Rebuild Reports Using Review Booklet

The Review Booklet offers a comprehensive method for report reconstruction, characterized by higher complexity and the necessity to redesign report layouts.

Firstly, similar to MDA, customers need to recreate a query in the new APP Custom Analytical Queries and Services.

DEMO

Then Navigate Review booklet tab in the App Manage KPIs and Reports to create to create a new review booklet.

As the purpose and layout of review booklet is different with multidimension report, customers need to redesign the report according to business needs.

Creating a Review Booklet in the Review Booklet Designer

While this demands significant time and consultant involvement, it assures regular updates, providing a future-proof solution for businesses with complex reporting needs.

Key Takeaway: Web Dynpro vs. MDA vs. Review Booklet

Each rebuilding strategy carries unique advantages:

  • Web Dynpro stands out for its simplicity but lacks future enhancements.
  • Review Booklet offers a robust solution with ongoing updates but requires high initial complexity.
  • MDA provides a balanced approach, coupling moderate complexity with up-to-date features and regular enhancements.(Recommanded)

 

Embracing change is essential in the journey towards more reliable, innovative reporting solutions. The transition from SAP Design Studio opens doors to enhanced analytical experiences and future-proof capabilities. This change is not just necessary, but a strategic advantage, setting up businesses for continued success in a data-driven world.

entry.title: Re: Is there any Configuration Procedure to enable Multip...
entry.content:
Hello,

if the test plans are created from Test Plan Management tile, you can define release status schema / release status values that require digital signature. The configuration is in tx SPRO - SAP Solution Manager - Test Suite - Test Suite Preparation - Setup - Specify Release Status Values / Set Up Release Status Schema.

A signature strategy can be assigned to each release status value, but this is useful only for REVIEW* values. See Signature Strategy in Customizing (SPRO) for SAP Solution Manager under Technical Settings -> Digital Signature.
If you set a release status value with a signature strategy, it starts immediately. The persons in the signature strategy must sign to fulfill the strategy. When the strategy is fulfilled, the assigned end status value is set automatically. If the signature strategy is cancelled, the assigned cancellation status value is set automatically.

See also Application Help Test Suite - Release Status Schema at: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/fbc7b5ecf5094fe0b6a2eb966160008f/5ab1cae0f3944d5dba6cc2b4c1a25441.html?locale=en-US

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: problem connecting process automation with build app
entry.content:
You need to perform three steps in order to see your process in the Build Apps:

1. RELEASE the process - you did it

2. Select RELEASED version of your process and DEPLOY  it - you haven't mentioned this step

3. PUBLISH the DEPLOYED version in the Build Library - you did it, but for process with nonexistent trigger

If you forgot to DEPLOY your process, the process trigger does not exist, so Build Apps can't see it.

entry.title: Re: How to assign roles to Consolidation steps ?
entry.content:
Hi T,

Yes, it is possible. In the customizing (direct below authorization group) MDCIMG -> Configure Process Template -> Specify Process Template,  you can assign the authorization group to your process template. First, select the process template what you use for your consolidation for example, then in the process step there is a column (the last one I think) and there you can assign authorization group. Then, only the user who assigned this authorization group can do the action.

Best regards,

Sungmin

  1. Download the edmx file from the Business Accelerator Hub
  2. import via "cds import <input_file> --as cds"
  3. Create srv/external/data/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER-A_BusinessPartner.csv and populate with example data from the docs
  4. Run the following code:
const bupa = await cds.connect.to('API_BUSINESS_PARTNER');
const { A_BusinessPartner } = bupa.entities;
const result = await bupa.run(SELECT(A_BusinessPartner).limit(100));

[cds] - mocking API_BUSINESS_PARTNER {
  impl: '../node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/common/Service.js',
  path: '/odata/v4/api-business-partner'
}

However, when the code above runs I'm getting the following error:

  code: '500',

    `SELECT json_insert(jsonb_insert('{}','$."BusinessPartner"',BusinessPartner,'$."Customer"'

entry.title: EWM Replenishment with HU Types and Packing Step – Error during Full HU Movement to Fixed Bin
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

we are currently facing a challenge in our SAP EWM replenishment process involving handling unit types (HU types) and process-oriented storage control (POSC). I’d like to outline the scenario and would appreciate any insights or recommendations from the community.

Use Case

We are implementing a replenishment process from a pallet storage type to a carton storage type, where the destination bin is a fixed bin that only allows smaller HU types (e.g. cartons).

To facilitate this, we’ve integrated a packing workstation into the process flow via process-oriented storage control (POSC). The idea is to:

  1. Trigger replenishment to the fixed bin.

  2. Create a warehouse task from the pallet storage type.

  3. Move the HU to the packing workstation.

  4. Repack the contents into a new HU (e.g. from pallet to carton).

  5. Putaway into the fixed bin.

System Behavior

  • Partial quantity warehouse tasks (i.e. not the full HU quantity) work perfectly:
    → The system creates the warehouse task, determines the packing work center, executes the repacking step, and completes replenishment to the fixed bin.

  • Full quantity movements (i.e. complete HU) lead to an error.
    Since pallet storage types typically require full HU movements, we have enabled rounding at storage type or warehouse process type level, so the system always tries to move the entire HU.

Issue

When the rounding results in a full HU removal, the warehouse task creation fails in transaction /SCWM/REPL with the following error:

/SCWM/PUT_BIN_DET055 - HU Type of the source HU is not allowed for the destination storage type.

 

It seems that during task creation, the system already validates the HU type against the final destination storage type (the fixed bin), even though the HU is not intended to reach the final bin in its original form – it should first go to the packing step, where it is repacked into an allowed HU type.

Question to the Community

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or knows how to handle HU type validation in replenishment scenarios involving POSC and full HU removal?

  • Is it possible to suppress the HU type check against the final bin during the initial WT creation if a packing step is part of the process?

  • Are we missing a configuration aspect in POSC, storage process definition, or warehouse process type?

  • How do you usually design replenishment with repacking via POSC, especially when dealing with full HUs and fixed bin restrictions?

Any ideas or best practices are highly appreciated.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Configure Relative Dates in Manage Route Maps for a 3...
entry.content:
Hi @MollyN 

A possible option is to set a number for the due date only. Based on Form Creation Date on the first step, and then on the following steps you can use as well Form Creation Date (so if step 2 is +7 days from step 1, then step 2 would have 14 days after Form Creation Date, and so on).  Or you can use the Previous Step Due Date as a reference to determine Step Due Date for the current step.

NOTE: In order to be able to save the changes, you will need to select an option for every date, even though it has to be left blank. I am adding a screenshot of what I am referring to, with the error and without the error. 

KBA that might be helpful as well 

2087333 - Route Maps - Start Date, Due Date, Exit Date, Enforce Start Date, and Automatic send on due date Validation - Performance Management https://me.sap.com/notes/2087333/E

Best is to test it and see if you get the expected results. 

No error

entry.title: Regex Filtering in File Name for SFTP Sender Adapter in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
SAP has quietly introduced Regex Filtering in the SFTP Sender Adapter, starting with adapter version 1.17 for SFTP Sender and version 1.18 for SFTP Poll Enrich.
Previously, although SAP documentation claimed regex support, it was not truly evaluated by the runtime. Only simple expressions using * and ? were interpreted:
* - matches zero or more characters
? - matches exactly one character

For example:
ab* matches: ab, abc, abcd
ab? matches: abc, ab1
This limitation created a challenge in SAP Process Orchestration (PO) to SAP Cloud Integration (CI) migration projects, where regex filtering in PO SFTP channels was already supported.

From SFTP adapter version 1.17 onward, a new checkbox - Regex Filtering - is available. Once enabled, the file name input field can evaluate actual regular expressions, bringing it in line with PO capabilities.
For example, to pick up files named abc.txt or 123.txt, you can now use:
^(abc\.txt|123\.txt)$

Old SFTP adapterNew SFTP adapter

 

 

If you are not Seeing the 'Regex Filtering' Option, it likely means you are using an older adapter version.
Solution: Delete the existing SFTP channel and create a new one using the latest adapter version.

FYI...You can take AI (ChatGPT, Copilot) help to come up with regex you need and use it in SFTP channel.
